summary: Gruber highlights Matthew 'mpt' Thomas's new blog post documenting his first 48 hours switching from Mac OS 9 to Mac OS X. Thomas, known as a thoughtful usability critic, compiled 48 specific criticisms of the OS X experience. Gruber endorses the piece as recommended reading, noting he only disagrees with 1.5 of Thomas's 48 points. The brief post serves as a signal boost for quality usability criticism of Apple's then-current operating system.
key_points: Matthew Thomas, a respected usability critic, switched from Mac OS 9 to OS X and documented 48 specific complaints
Gruber finds Thomas's criticisms almost entirely valid, disagreeing with only 1.5 out of 48 points
Gruber values informed, detailed usability criticism of Apple products even when it's largely negative
money_quotes: It's always fun when a thoughtful usability critic switches from Mac OS 9 to OS X.
Out of his 48 points, I disagree with one and a half — all of #28, and the parenthetical aside in #3.
Recommended reading, even if you wind up disagreeing with more than 1.5 of his criticisms.
key_insight: Gruber's near-total agreement with 48 usability criticisms of Mac OS X shows his willingness to acknowledge Apple's design shortcomings when the critique is well-reasoned.
